app.title: Celestial Mechanics — an interactive n-body simulation

force.heading: Softened gravitational force
force.body: Every pair of bodies (i, j) pulls on one another. A small softening ε is added to prevent the force blowing up when bodies approach — physically it smears each mass into a tiny Plummer sphere.
force.footnote: Click any coloured symbol in the formula to jump to its slider. n = 2 is our universe; n = 1 gives unstable spirals, n > 2 gives precessing rosettes.

integrator.heading: Velocity-Verlet integration
integrator.body: A second-order symplectic scheme — small energy drift over long runs, unlike naive Euler which spirals away.
integrator.footnote: Smaller Δt is more accurate but the simulation plays back slower. Try cranking Δt up to see the integrator break down.

energy.heading: Total energy
energy.body: For a closed system this should be conserved. The drift meter below shows how much our integrator is cheating.

masses.heading: Body masses
masses.body: Each preset starts with tuned initial conditions. Nudge a mass and watch the orbits decay, precess, or eject.
